claim: Smaller parameter-efficient models (30B with 3B active) can match or exceed larger models (1T) on reasoning benchmarks through mixture-of-experts and specialized training
actionable: Evaluate MiroThinker 1.5 or similar small MoE models for local agentic deployments to balance performance with inference cost and latency

claim: Context retention and memory management systems built into models (recency-based truncation) are becoming standard for handling long agent interactions
actionable: Implement context compaction and recency-based retention in agent loops to prevent context window overflow during multi-turn interactions

claim: Agent tool-calling capability is more valuable than raw parameter count for practical agentic applications (400+ tool calls vs. model size)
actionable: Prioritize models with strong tool-calling capabilities and long max iterations over largest available models when building practical agents

claim: Quantized versions (4-bit, 8-bit MLX) of specialized agentic models retain reasoning quality while enabling local deployment on consumer hardware
actionable: Test 4-bit and 8-bit quantized versions of models locally using MLX or llama.cpp to find practical tradeoffs between performance and hardware requirements
